control_card.C: One Python stdlib script writes JSON, CSV, Markdown, and manifest artifacts.
control_card.F: General 3D remains open because nonlocal pressure, vortex-stretching alignment, and sign-definite defect flux are not closed without the symmetry hypotheses.
control_card.G: Symmetric machinery can support scoped axisymmetric-with-swirl rows; general smooth finite-energy 3D Navier-Stokes remains guarded false.
control_card.L: D_r monotonicity would need a closed local sign ledger, pressure control, and alignment persistence outside the symmetric class.
control_card.O: Lane 3 owns the Sprint161 defect-monotonicity gap emitter.
control_card.P: Use this as a normalized obstruction ledger, not as a monotonicity theorem.
control_card.R: Normalize the D_r monotonicity obstruction: local defect energy, needed sign/pressure/alignment hypotheses, symmetric support, and the remaining general 3D gap.
control_card.S: Sprint157-160 isolate a symmetric Hou-Luo publication packet while keeping full Clay and mechanism-exhaustion promotion false.

local_defect_energy_D_r: D_r(t,x0) = normalized localized defect/enstrophy-energy excess on a parabolic ball Q_r(t,x0), compared against the scale-compatible dissipation and flux budget.
why_general_3d_remains_open: The current symmetric machinery does not provide a sign-definite D_r inequality, a critical-scale nonlocal pressure closure, or persistent vortex-stretching alignment for arbitrary smooth finite-energy 3D data.
